notes: big delivery day today. first truck arrived 6:30 with 4 pallets of common bricks, about 500 per pallet so 2000 bricks total. checked the delivery docket matches our PO, all good

second delivery at 7:15, timber framing package from Bowens. 60 lengths of 90 by 45 MGP10 at 4.8 metres, 30 lengths of 90 by 35 MGP10 at 3.6 metres, and 20 sheets of 15 mil plywood for bracing. counted them all off the truck, correct quantities
theres also 2 packs of truss nail plates in that delivery. 200 plates per pack. stored them in the container cos they'll rust if they get wet

oh the plywood has some water damage on like 3 sheets. the plastic wrap was torn on the top of the pack. gonna use those ones for temporary bracing not permanent. noted it on the delivery docket and took photos
brickies used about 600 bricks today plus 15 bags of mortar mix. we started the day with 40 bags of mortar so we've got 25 left. need to reorder, gonna need at least another 50 bags for next week
the sand pile is getting low too. probably only 2 cubic metres left. need a delivery of brickies sand, the washed stuff not the yellow rubbish we got last time
electrician used 3 rolls of 2.5mm TPS cable today running circuits on level 1. hes got maybe 1 and a half rolls left from the original order of 10 rolls. need to reorder cable
also the sparky needs more 20mm conduit. used about 40 lengths of the 4m sticks this week. ordering another bundle of 50
plumber brought his own materials today. 50m of 100mm PVC DWV pipe, about 30 fittings various types, glue and primer. his stuff not ours
reo delivery confirmed for tomorrow morning. 2 tonnes of N12 bar and 500kg of N16 bar. also 50 ligature sets for the column cages. got the mill certs emailed through already
we burned through heaps of screws today with the framing. used probably 4 boxes of 75mm batten screws. only 2 boxes left on the shelf. adding to the order list

the render sand that was delivered yesterday is the wrong grade. ordered M classification but they sent U classification. way too coarse for the finish coat. called the supplier, theyre picking it up tomorrow and replacing it. in the meantime the renderers cant do the top coat, only the scratch coat
concrete sealer arrived. 4 drums of Duram Duraseal, 20 litres each. thats enough for the ground floor slab and the driveway. stored in the hazmat cage
waterproofing membrane rolls delivered. 6 rolls of Ardex WPM 300, each roll is 20m x 1m. need 5 rolls for the bathrooms and laundry so we've got 1 spare which is handy

we're completely out of 75mm nails for the framing gun. borrowed a box off the chippy next door but need to get our own supply sorted. ordered 5 boxes from the trade store, should arrive tomorrow on the morning run
roof tiles were delivered this arvo. 28 pallets of Monier concrete tiles, terracotta colour. thats the full amount for the whole roof. stacked em in the car park area with plastic over the top
counted the DPC (damp proof course) rolls. got 6 rolls left which should be enough for the remaining walls. each roll is 30m and we've got about 150 lineal metres of wall left so yeah should be fine
waste today: about half a skip of timber offcuts, a bucket of bent nails, and a few cracked bricks. not bad actually, pretty minimal waste compared to last week
